description: Classify San Francisco planning zones into "parcelization grain" categories based on average parcel size and assess how urban forms impact density using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/parcelization_morphology.ipynb. Process the 'parcels', 'buildings', and 'jobs' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Exclude parcels with missing or zero acreage. Aggregate data by zone_id to calculate total parcels, average parcel acres, total parcel acres, total residential units, and total jobs (filling missing values with 0). Filter for zones with at least 20 parcels. Assign a grain_category based on average parcel acres: 'Fine-Grain' (< 0.15), 'Medium-Grain' (0.15 to < 0.5), 'Coarse-Grain' (0.5 to < 2.0), and 'Superblock' (>= 2.0). Calculate units_per_acre and jobs_per_acre for each zone. Export the zone-level results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_morphology.csv. Generate a summary of mean densities grouped by grain_category and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/grain_summary.csv. Finally, produce a grouped bar chart of the summary data and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/morphology_density_chart.png. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
